title: Improving Transparency into Pharmacy Benefit Manager Fee Disclosure

supporting_related_material.title: Proposed Rule, Extension of Comment Period - Improving Transparency Into Pharmacy Benefit Manager Fee Disclosure

comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: I support the Department of Labor's proposed rule requiring Pharmacy Benefit Managers (PBMs) to disclose their compensation to employer and union health plans. I pay a significant amount every month for health insurance premiums, prescriptions, and other healthcare costs. Yet it often feels impossible to understand where that money is actually going. I strongly support the Department of Labor’s proposed rule to improve transparency into pharmacy benefit manager compensation and to require meaningful fee disclosures to ERISA plan fiduciaries. I. This Rule Addresses a Genuine and Long-Standing Market Failure. Pharmacy Benefit Managers occupy a central position in America's prescription drug supply chain — negotiating drug prices, setting formularies, processing claims, and controlling access to pharmacy networks — yet they have operated for decades with virtually no obligation to disclose how they...

CMS needs to accelerate the move to FHIR based attachments. CMS needs to accelerate the transitions from X12 to FHIR, CDA to FHIR, and V2 to FHIR in record time to remain competitive with EHDS by 2035 or the latest by 2037. The continued dependence on X12 and CDA is delaying CMS’s ability to see PBM fees in real time and PBM related workflow data.

FHIR based interoperability for pharmacy can enhance fee transparency for PBM disclosure and has the potential to improve quality outcomes via the MLR quality improvement activities for PDPs. Pharmacy workflows for real time stock availability, fill status updates to prescribers, and pharmacy-to-pharmacy are still not ubiquitous.
There needs to be a MLR % payment breakdown as given below: MLR % paid to PBMs MLR % paid to first tier, downstream, and related entities (FDRs i.e. delegated entities) MLR % paid to clinicians

Updated comment MLR percentage needs to be broken down further: MLR % paid to PBMs MLR % paid to delegated entities (including TPAs, MCOs, specialty service vendors) MLR % actually given to clinicians
